mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in greater than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 200, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 200 consistently coincides with positive 20-day forward returns, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces negative 20-day returns in greater than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines greater than 0.5% fail to precede negative 5-day returns in greater than 55% of observations.
